prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of the current year of our lord, the software assemblers did cry out to the heavens, proclaiming a bounty of coin that filled the coffers unto overflowing. The high priestess of marketing did cast wide her spells across the land, and verily did triple the crops of subscriptions which did feed the teeming user multitudes. The scribes of code did toil night and day in the candlelit crypt beneath the hollowed oak, weaving arcane incantations to conjure forth new miracles to feast the eyes of the onlookers. And in the final reckoning, these purveyors of the heavenly scroll did record a great multitude of beasts of burden laden with tribute - by conservative count, not less than a grand mountain of silver shillings, as bright as the sun and as plentiful as the stars in their firmament.
